stories.title: Lucas Glover Leads John Deere Classic at -14 — Two-Shot Edge Into Round 3

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Lucas Glover through 36 holes at the John Deere Classic:

⛳ -14 total (63+65)
⛳ Zero bogeys
⛳ 14 birdies, 22 pars
⛳ 2-shot lead
⛳ Best 36-hole total in 582 PGA Tour starts

Bogey-free in the Heartland. Happy 4th. 🇺🇸 #JohnDeereClassic

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Lucas Glover leads the John Deere Classic at -14 heading into Round 3 today.

No bogeys in 36 holes.

If he wins Sunday, it'll be the best performance of his career at the perfect place — America's holiday, America's heartland.

Follow the action. 🏌️ #JohnDeereClassic

facebook.long_form:
🏆 LUCAS GLOVER BOGEY-FREE THROUGH 36 HOLES — LEADS JOHN DEERE CLASSIC AT -14

Happy Fourth of July from TPC Deere Run in Silvis, Illinois, where Lucas Glover is putting on one of the great performances of his career at the John Deere Classic.

Through two rounds, Glover is at 14-under par (63+65=128), leading the field by two shots and carrying the best 36-hole total of his 582 PGA Tour career starts. The most remarkable part? He has not made a single bogey. Fourteen birdies. Twenty-two pars. Not one dropped shot in 36 holes.

The leaderboard through 36 holes:
🥇 Lucas Glover — -14 (2-shot lead)
🥈 Lee Hodges — -12
Others: Max Homa, Chris Gotterup, Zac Blair all chasing

For Glover, a win this week would be one of the signature moments of a PGA Tour career that now spans 582 starts. The veteran was asked if he was thinking about the streak. "I'm not thinking about bogeys or not," he said. "I'm just playing golf."

Round 3 is today — Saturday, July 4. If Glover stays bogey-free, he will be the story of the Fourth of July holiday weekend in professional golf.
facebook.image_caption: Lucas Glover at -14, zero bogeys in 36 holes. The John Deere Classic is his to lose. 🏆 #JohnDeereClassic #PGATour

article.headline: Lucas Glover Bogey-Free at -14: The John Deere Classic Leader Is Playing Flawless Golf on July 4

stories.title: Amateur Preston Stout Contending at John Deere — World No. 1 Amateur Makes a Statement

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Preston Stout — world No. 1 amateur, reigning NCAA champion — is CONTENDING at the John Deere Classic.

4th PGA Tour start. Made the cut. T11 through 36 holes.

He outplayed Jordan Spieth in round 1.

The kids are not alright — they're coming for everyone. 🏌️ #JohnDeereClassic

facebook.long_form:
⭐ THE WORLD'S No. 1 AMATEUR IS CONTENDING AT THE JOHN DEERE CLASSIC

Preston Stout is not supposed to be here. Not this year, not this weekend, not at this level.

He is the reigning NCAA individual champion from Oklahoma State and the new No. 1 amateur in the world — a ranking he claimed after Jackson Koivun turned professional. This is just his fourth PGA Tour start. Professional golfers spend years getting comfortable at this level. Stout looks completely at home.

Through 36 holes at the John Deere Classic, Stout made the cut for the first time in his four-event PGA Tour career and sits tied 11th. In round one, his play was better than two-time tournament champion Jordan Spieth's and J.T. Poston's — both of them proven winners on Tour.

His group at T11 includes past champion Zach Johnson, multiple winners Chris Gotterup and Ben Griffin. And the world's No. 1 amateur.

On Independence Day weekend, at a tournament that celebrates America's heartland, a college kid from Oklahoma State is making the professionals uncomfortable. The Fourth of July always has room for one great unexpected story — and Preston Stout might be it.
facebook.image_caption: World No. 1 amateur Preston Stout is contending at the John Deere Classic in just his 4th PGA Tour start. The kids are coming. ⭐ #JohnDeereClassic

article.headline: Preston Stout Is Contending at the John Deere Classic: The World's No. 1 Amateur Is Ready for the Pros

stories.title: LIV Golf Funding Crisis Deepens — CEO Won't Guarantee Final 4 Events of 2026

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
The LIV Golf CEO was asked if he could guarantee the final 4 events of 2026 would be played.

He refused to say yes.

Saudi PIF has only funded ~$200M of the $600M it committed. The tour needs $350M in new investment.

LIV Golf is in survival mode. #LIVGolf #Golf

facebook.long_form:
⚠️ LIV GOLF FUNDING CRISIS: CEO WON'T GUARANTEE FINAL 4 EVENTS OF 2026

The LIV Golf League is facing a financial crisis that threatens to end its 2026 season early.

At the center of the problem: money. LIV Golf needs approximately $400 million to cover player contracts, prize purses, and operating costs for the remainder of the 2026 season. Saudi Arabia's sovereign wealth fund, the Public Investment Fund (PIF), has only provided around $200 million — roughly a third of the $600 million it committed.

In April, PIF made it official: it would fund LIV only through the end of the 2026 season, saying "the substantial investment required by LIV Golf over a longer term is no longer consistent with the current phase of PIF's investment strategy."

When LIV CEO Scott O'Neil was asked directly whether he could guarantee the final four events of 2026 would take place, he refused to say yes. Instead he told reporters: "What I can guarantee is that there is a heck of a return if you come and invest in this business." He has 18 investor meetings scheduled in the search for $350 million in new private capital.

For the players — many of whom left the PGA Tour for enormous guaranteed contracts — this is not a theoretical concern. The next LIV event is July 23-26 in England. Whether they all get there is now an open question.
facebook.image_caption: LIV Golf CEO won't guarantee the final 4 events of 2026. The funding crisis is real and it's getting worse. ⚠️ #LIVGolf #Golf

article.headline: LIV Golf's Funding Crisis: CEO Won't Guarantee the Final Four Events of 2026 Will Happen

stories.title: Evian Championship Preview — LPGA's Fifth Major Arrives in France on July 9

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
One week from today, the LPGA heads to France for its 5th major of 2026.

🏆 The Amundi Evian Championship
📍 Evian Resort Golf Club, Evian-les-Bains, France
🗓️ July 9-12
💰 $9.10 million purse

The French Alps await. Who's your pick? 🏌️‍♀️ #LPGA #EvianChampionship

facebook.long_form:
🏔️ LPGA'S FIFTH MAJOR OF 2026 ARRIVES IN FRANCE NEXT WEEK — EVIAN CHAMPIONSHIP PREVIEW

One week from today — July 9 — the LPGA heads to the French Alps for one of the most beautiful settings in professional golf.

The Amundi Evian Championship runs July 9-12 at Evian Resort Golf Club in Evian-les-Bains, France. It is the fifth and final major championship of the 2026 LPGA season, with a purse of $9.10 million.

The tournament has been a launching pad for first-time major winners in recent years. In 2025, Grace Kim claimed her breakthrough major title at Evian — the third consecutive first-time major winner to win at the course.

The Evian Championship occupies a unique place on the LPGA calendar. The lake setting, the French Alps backdrop, and the Resort Golf Club create a visual drama unlike any other event on Tour. For the players, it's a bucket-list week even before the major championship status is factored in.

With the top players in women's golf descending on the south shore of Lake Geneva in five days, the question of who breaks through at Evian in 2026 is the LPGA's next great storyline.
facebook.image_caption: The Amundi Evian Championship begins July 9 in France. $9.1M purse. Fifth LPGA major of 2026. The countdown is on. 🏔️ #LPGA #EvianChampionship

article.headline: Evian Championship Preview: LPGA's Fifth Major Heads to the French Alps July 9-12

stories.title: Scheffler Holds World No. 1 — The King's Court Sits Out John Deere

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Scottie Scheffler: not playing the John Deere Classic this week.

Still the world No. 1. Still leading the FedExCup with 3,511 points.

He lost to Hovland in a playoff at the Travelers.

His ranking didn't move.

That's how dominant he's been all year. 🏌️ #PGATour #Golf

facebook.long_form:
🌍 SCOTTIE SCHEFFLER — STILL THE WORLD'S BEST GOLFER

Scottie Scheffler is not playing the John Deere Classic this week. The Fourth of July tournament in the Midwest is not on his schedule. He is taking the holiday weekend off.

And yet he is still the No. 1 player in the world. Barely a month into the second half of the PGA Tour season, Scheffler remains atop the Official World Golf Ranking as of the June 29, 2026 update — a position he has held for more than 175 weeks across his career.

The most recent evidence of his dominance: Scheffler finished runner-up at the Travelers Championship at the end of June, shooting a final round that would have won in most years. Viktor Hovland beat him in a Monday playoff. Scheffler's ranking didn't move.

That is the definition of a player whose ranking is immovable — the margin between him and the field is so wide that even finishing second to a player who shot -21 for the week doesn't cost him a single position.

Scheffler leads the FedExCup standings with 3,511 points. The four-time major champion returns to action after the July 4 weekend with Open Championship at Royal Birkdale (July 16-19) as the next marquee target.
facebook.image_caption: Scottie Scheffler: sitting out the John Deere, still No. 1 in the world. Nobody can touch him right now. 🌍 #PGATour #Golf

article.headline: Scottie Scheffler Takes July 4 Weekend Off — And He's Still the Best Golfer in the World
